title: Build Agentic Optimizations that Cut Supply Chain Planning from Weeks to Minutes
description:
Building an advanced superchip like the NVIDIA Blackwell means coordinating over a million parts. Demand shifts weekly, but supply chain decisions (e.g., sourcing) must be locked in months ahead. At that scale, traditional planning can fall short — weeks of manual work, compounding complexity, and exponential risk with every decision.

NVIDIA applied an AI-first planner to its own internal supply

title: Jan 13:  Jetson AI Lab Research Group Call - Accelerating Robotics with Isaac ROS on Jetson
description: Accelerating Robotics with Isaac ROS on Jetson

title: Build Optimized Supply Chains with NVIDIA cuOpt Agents and Skills
description:
Learn how to run the NVIDIA cuOpt Supply Chain Agent — an open-source reference architecture inspired by NVIDIA's own AI-first supply chain planner, built for global-scale logistics optimization.
In this tutorial, you'll deploy the agent using the BREV launchable, which sets up a preconfigured environment with cuOpt and all required dependencies. From there, the Jupyter notebook walks through init

title: Designing a Modular 6G System Using NVIDIA Aerial™ Framework
description:
Michael Roe, NVIDIA Principal Engineer, presents on NVIDIA Aerial™ Framework at NVIDIA 6G Developer Day 2025. 

Learn how to get started with the new, modular Aerial Framework for writing 6G signal blocks and pipelines.

➡️ Learn more: https://docs.nvidia.com/aerial/framework/latest/overview/index.html

description:

Learn how to prototype AI-native RAN algorithms under real-time 5G constraints using a GPU-accelerated research platform with over-the-air testing capabilities.

title: Generating Performant 6G GPU-Accelerated Code From High-Level Programming Languages

title: Building AI Factories With Digital Twins | OpenUSD Insider
description:
AI factories are scaling at unprecedented speed, but designing and deploying them efficiently remains a major challenge. In this session, NVIDIA and Jacobs will showcase how digital twins powered by NVIDIA Omniverse DSX help teams design, simulate, optimize, and validate AI infrastructure before construction begins. 

Learn how virtual facilities can accelerate deployment timelines, improve energy

title: How to Train AI Robots with Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab | Robotics Office Hours
description:
Join NVIDIA for Robotics Office Hours, the weekly livestream for developers building AI-powered robots. In this session, NVIDIA experts share an end-to-end robot learning workflow using Isaac Sim, Isaac Lab, NVIDIA Cosmos, and NVIDIA Omniverse to train manipulation policies and achieve sim-to-real transfer.

Learn why end-to-end learning is gaining momentum, how it compares to traditional robotics

description:

title: Living Together, Learning Together: Inside Zurich's Inclusive Wheelchair Flatshare
description:
Discover this inclusive flatshare, where ETH Zurich students and wheelchair users live together, learn from each other, and break down everyday barriers.

For many, the world’s inaccessibility is invisible — until it affects you personally. A resident shares what it was like to go outside in a wheelchair for the first time and how living in this unique shared home helps people with paraplegia rebu

title: Task-Oriented Robot-Human Handovers on Legged Manipulators
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description: We present AFT-Handover, a framework that integrates large language model (LLM)-driven affordance reasoning with texture-based affordance transfer to achieve zero-shot, task-oriented robot-to-human handovers. In a comparative user study, our framework is preferred over the current state-of-the-art by 71.43% of the participants, reducing human regrasping effort and enhancing perceived task understa

Authors: Casimir Feldmann, Maximum Wilder-Smith, Vaishakh Patil, Michael Oechsle, Michael Niemeyer, Keisuke Tateno, and Marco Hutter

Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have demonstrated impressi

title: Large-Scale Autonomous Gas Monitoring for Volcanic Environments: A Legged Robot on Mount Etna
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description:
Volcanic gas measurements are critical for understanding eruptive activity. However, harsh terrain, hazardous conditions, and logistical constraints make near-surface data collection extremely challenging.  
In this work, we present an autonomous legged robotic system for volcanic gas monitoring, validated through real-world deployments on Mount Etna. The system combines a quadruped robot equipped

title: Spatially-Enhanced Recurrent Memory for Long-Range Mapless Navigation
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description:
Can recurrent neural networks really understand space? We discovered that standard RNNs like LSTMs and GRUs excel at capturing time, but struggle with space.

We're introducing Spatially-Enhanced Recurrent Units (SRUs) — a simple yet powerful modification that enables robots to build implicit spatial memories for navigation. Published in the International Journal of Robotics Research (IJRR), this

description: ▶ Full film: https://youtu.be/ElE87bS41uI

title: What If Evolution Was Metal Instead of Carbon?
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description:
What if evolution wasn’t carbon-based — but metal instead?
This short film explores an alternative, iron-based evolution through robots, simulation, and real-world machines.

Inspired by biological evolution, this Christmas lab film imagines a world where machines evolve instead of organisms. 
From code and simulation to real robots, we follow an accelerated, fictional evolution — driven by optimi

title: Robotic World Model: A Neural Network Simulator for Robust Policy Optimization in Robotics
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description: Learning robust and generalizable world models is crucial for enabling efficient and scalable robotic control in real-world environments. In this work, we introduce a novel framework for learning world models that accurately capture complex, partially observable, and stochastic dynamics. The proposed method employs a dual-autoregressive mechanism and self-supervised training to achieve reliable lo

title: Divide, Discover, Deploy: Factorized Skill Learning with Symmetry and Style Priors
channel: Robotic Systems Lab: Legged Robotics at ETH Zürich
description: Unsupervised Skill Discovery (USD) allows agents to autonomously learn diverse behaviors without task-specific rewards. While recent USD methods have shown promise, their application to real-world robotics remains underexplored. In this paper, we propose a modular USD framework to address the challenges in the safety, interpretability, and deployability of the learned skills. Our approach employs

title: Sybil: An AI model that can predict lung cancer risk 6 years in advance
description:
Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer-related deaths worldwide. While smoking is the leading cause of lung cancer, lung cancer rates among nonsmokers are on the rise.

Every person has the right to know their risk of developing cancer — Sybil is a deep learning model that accurately predicts a patient’s risk of lung cancer up to six years in advance from analyzing a low-dose CT scan, ensuring

title: AI+Science: Accelerating Discovery
description:
Experts will separate hype from reality, spotlighting where AI is already enabling genuine breakthroughs and where its limits and risks remain.

Centered on the essential role of scientists in this new era, the conference will explore both near-term advances and future possibilities, along with the unique challenges of applying AI in scientific work. Above all, it focuses on elevating human unders

title: HAI Seminar: Predicting Child Labor in Ghana's Cocoa Industry
description:
Child labor remains prevalent in Ghana’s cocoa sector and is associated with adverse educational and health outcomes for children.

In this HAI seminar, civil engineering PhD student, Antonio Skillicorn, presented their study on identifying key predictors of child labor risk. They investigated whether district-level satellite indicators, including yield-weighted cocoa-driven deforestation, newly l

title: HAI Seminar: Learning by Creating – A Human-Centered Vision for AI in Education
description:
Generative AI is entering classrooms at a breathtaking pace, often presented as a solution for efficiency by automating tasks that risk promoting a shallow, transmission-oriented model of education where teachers become content moderators, students become prompt engineers, and learning collapses into producing answers rather than developing understanding.  

In this HAI seminar, HAI faculty fellow

title: HAI Seminar: Nurturing Africa’s AI Leaders through Math Olympiad
description:
The African Olympiad Academy is a world-class high school dedicated to training Africa’s most promising students in mathematics, science, and artificial intelligence through olympiad-based pedagogy.

In this HAI seminar, AOA Founders discussed their approach to identifying and developing top-tier AI talent on the African continent in coordination with complementary initiatives such as Black in AI.

title: AI+Education Summit 2026: Closing Session – From Possibility to Progress
AI+Education Summit 2026: How AI Is Transforming How We Connect, Collaborate, and Grow
description: Learning is fundamentally social, and AI is beginning to reshape how we connect, collaborate, and grow together. This session explored how AI can support richer interactions, strengthen relationships, and expand learning and wellbeing when designed with human connection at the center, while also examining the risks of isolation, over-automation, and weakened social bonds if we get it wrong.

title: AI+Education Summit 2026: AI Quests – When Learning Sciences Meet Product Design toward AI Literacy
description: This session explored how educators and learners are bridging the gap between rapid AI innovation and public understanding. Google Research and Stanford Accelerator for Learning expanded AI literacy using immersive, real-world experiences and sound pedagogy. They turned complex research into age-appropriate lessons, emphasizing decision-making and ethics in AI.

title: AI+Education Summit 2026: Blueprints for a Global Human-Centered Learning System
AI+Education Summit 2026: Create+AI Challenge Ceremony
AI+Education Summit 2026: How AI is Transforming How We Teach
description: This session highlighted grounded, real-world stories of how educators and learners are navigating AI—what’s opening up, what’s challenging, and what’s needed next.

title: The To Do List with Spot | Boston Dynamics
description:
For an industrial robot built for the rigors of factories and power plants, tidying up a living room may seem like a light day at the office for Spot. Yet, this demonstration represents the promise of AI models in robotics. In this case, Google’s visual-language model (VLM) Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 was empowering Spot with embodied reasoning. 

Go behind the scenes and watch the full demo in our blo

title: How do you make a reliable backflip? | Boston Dynamics

title: Form & Function of Enterprise Humanoid Design | Boston Dynamics Tech Talk | Atlas
description: The production version of Atlas is a departure from the typical humanoid form factor, favoring industrial utility over human likeness. Intended for purposeful work in an industrial setting, Atlas has a form factor that signals its role as a machine rather than a companion or friendly assistant. Join two lead hardware engineers and our head of industrial design for a technical discussion of how key

title: What's your favorite classic Boston Dynamics robot? #bostondynamics #robot
description: Two of our product managers share why they love Sandflea and BigDog, and the legacy of innovation inspiring the next wave of robotics solutions.

title: ST Engineering MRAS | Boston Dynamics
description:
Data powers an organization's digital transformation, and ST Engineering MRAS is leveraging Spot to get a full view of critical equipment and facility. Working autonomously, Spot collects information about machine health - and now, thanks to an integration of the Leica BLK ARC for reality capture, detailed and accurate point cloud data for their digital twin. 

Learn more: https://bostondynamics.c

title: Spot at Cargill | Customer Testimonial Excerpt
description:
Cargill’s Amsterdam Multiseed facility enlists Spot and Orbit to inspect machinery and perform visual checks, enhanced by all-new AI features, as part of their “Plant of the Future” program. 

Learn more about how Spot helps Cargill optimize operations on the ground: https://bostondynamics.com/case-studies/spot-at-cargill/

title: Great Honor to Welcome German Chancellor Merz
description:
It was a great honor to welcome German Chancellor and his delegation to visit Unitree Robotics！
We sincerely appreciate the enthusiasm from German entrepreneurs for Unitree robots！
We look forward to working with more outstanding companies around the world to achieve mutual success,
and to jointly advance intelligent robotics technology for the benefit of all humanity.

title: TiDAR: Think in Diffusion, Talk in Autoregression (Paper Analysis)
description:
Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08923

Abstract:
Diffusion language models hold the promise of fast parallel generation, while autoregressive (AR) models typically excel in quality due to their causal structure aligning naturally with language modeling. This raises a fundamental question: can we achieve a synergy with high throughput, higher GPU utilization, and AR level quality? Existing method

title: Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time (Paper Analysis)
description:
Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00663

Abstract:
Over more than a decade there has been an extensive research effort on how to effectively utilize recurrent models and attention. While recurrent models aim to compress the data into a fixed-size memory (called hidden state), attention allows attending to the entire context window, capturing the direct dependencies of all tokens. This more accurat

title: [Paper Analysis] The Free Transformer (and some Variational Autoencoder stuff)
description:
https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.17558

Abstract:
We propose an extension of the decoder Transformer that conditions its generative process on random latent variables which are learned without supervision thanks to a variational procedure. Experimental evaluations show that allowing such a conditioning translates into substantial improvements on downstream tasks.

Author: François Fleuret

Homepa

title: Context Rot: How Increasing Input Tokens Impacts LLM Performance (Paper Analysis)
description:
Paper: https://research.trychroma.com/context-rot

Abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) are typically presumed to process context uniformly—that is, the model should handle the 10,000th token just as reliably as the 100th. However, in practice, this assumption does not hold. We observe that model performance varies significantly as input length changes, even on simple tasks.
In this report, we e

Website: https://energy-based-transformers.github.io/

Abstract:
Inference-time computation techniques, analogous to human System 2 Thinking, have recently become popular for improving model performances. However, most existing approaches suffer from several limitations: they are modality-specific (e.g., working only in
